reasoning: The reference class is 1-year expected inflation rate estimates from the Cleveland Fed. The June 2025 value is 2.38%, which is below the 1-year historical mean of ~2.53%. Looking at the last 13 months, 8 out of 13 data points were above the 2.38% threshold, indicating that this is a relatively low base and a given month typically has a >50% chance of exceeding it. The series is highly volatile (e.g. jumping from 2.18 in March to 2.89 in April), making swift upward mean-reverting adjustments plausible. In addition, the statistical linear tool forecasts a robust probability (0.67) of being above the threshold in the short term. While recent momentum has been downward, the low starting value outweighs this. Following instructions, uncertainty increases over time, meaning probabilities for more distant dates are drawn closer to 0.50 (0.67 -> 0.61 -> 0.56).
